opportunities.title: Approved-answer questionnaire workspace
opportunities.problem: Repeated questionnaires delay enterprise deals and consume reviewer time.
opportunities.current_spend_or_workaround: Teams coordinate spreadsheets, documents, chat, and repeated expert review.
opportunities.mechanism: Reuse approved answers with source provenance, ownership, expiry, and reviewer controls.
opportunities.why_now: Smaller SaaS vendors face expanding enterprise security review without enterprise tooling budgets.

contradiction.summary: Trust requirements may favor incumbent platforms and human review.

assumptions.statement: Target teams receive enough questionnaires for reuse to matter.

risks.statement: Buyers may distrust generated or stale answers.
risks.mitigation: Require provenance, expiry, and explicit reviewer approval.

validation_experiment.hypothesis: Teams will pay to reduce answer lookup and coordination time.
validation_experiment.method: Run a reviewer-controlled concierge workflow for five design partners.
validation_experiment.metric: Paid pilot commitments and median response time
validation_experiment.success_criteria: Three paid pilots and a 30 percent response-time reduction

opportunities.builder_fit: A small technical team can build the bounded ingestion, search, and approval workflow in six weeks.
opportunities.distribution_channel: Security-lead and B2B SaaS operator communities
opportunities.distribution_rationale: The identifiable buyer already gathers in focused practitioner groups and vendor networks.
opportunities.defensibility: Approved-answer history, integrations, and reviewer trust compound with continued use.
opportunities.technical_feasibility: The MVP needs document ingestion, structured answer records, provenance, search, and workflow state.
opportunities.operational_burden: Human review and source freshness checks remain necessary.

evidence_strength.rationale: Two synthetic independent sources support recurrence, cost, and contradiction.

novelty.rationale: The wedge is reviewer-controlled reuse rather than generic answer generation.

builder_fit.rationale: The bounded workflow fits a small technical team and six-week horizon.

technical_feasibility.rationale: The MVP uses conventional document and workflow components.

distribution_clarity.rationale: Focused buyer communities provide an initial channel.

operational_burden.rationale: Review and evidence freshness cannot be fully automated.

opportunities.title: Questionnaire response concierge
opportunities.problem: Small teams need temporary help coordinating complex questionnaires.
opportunities.current_spend_or_workaround: Founders and security staff interrupt roadmap work to coordinate responses.
opportunities.mechanism: Combine lightweight workflow software with a time-boxed reviewer concierge.
opportunities.why_now: Enterprise security review is reaching vendors before they can justify broad compliance tooling.

contradiction.summary: Service delivery may require too much bespoke judgment.
contradiction.evidence_gap: No independent willingness-to-pay evidence for a concierge service.

assumptions.statement: A standardized workflow can keep service margins acceptable.

risks.statement: Bespoke review can overwhelm a small operator.
risks.mitigation: Limit scope and measure exception rates before productizing.

validation_experiment.hypothesis: Teams will pay per questionnaire for coordination relief.
validation_experiment.method: Offer five fixed-scope paid concierge pilots.
validation_experiment.success_criteria: Two paid pilots under eight delivery hours each

opportunities.builder_fit: The service can validate demand before deeper automation.
opportunities.distribution_rationale: The pain appears during active enterprise deals where peer referrals are credible.
opportunities.defensibility: Accumulated workflow templates may improve delivery, but service defensibility is initially weak.
opportunities.technical_feasibility: A lightweight internal workspace is sufficient for the pilot.
opportunities.operational_burden: Manual coordination and expert review create high ongoing burden.

evidence_strength.rationale: The service format lacks independent willingness-to-pay evidence.

novelty.rationale: Concierge delivery is a validation wedge rather than a novel product.

builder_fit.rationale: It is quick to start but adds service work.

technical_feasibility.rationale: Little software is required for the first test.

distribution_clarity.rationale: Referrals can reach teams in active deals.

opportunities.title: Approved-answer integration API
opportunities.problem: Existing deal and compliance systems cannot reliably retrieve current approved answers.
opportunities.current_spend_or_workaround: Teams copy answers manually between documents and systems.
opportunities.mechanism: Expose approved answers and provenance through a narrow integration API.
opportunities.why_now: More questionnaire tools create pressure for an interoperable source of approved answers.

contradiction.summary: Integration trust and incumbent APIs may erase the wedge.
contradiction.evidence_gap: No direct evidence that buyers want a standalone API.

assumptions.statement: Teams prefer integration over a complete workspace.

risks.statement: Incumbent platforms may restrict useful integration access.
risks.mitigation: Validate two specific integration paths before building broadly.

validation_experiment.hypothesis: Two teams will commit to one approved-answer integration.
validation_experiment.method: Test a read-only API mock with five security teams.

opportunities.builder_fit: A read-only API prototype is technically bounded.
opportunities.distribution_rationale: Partners already reach teams with fragmented security workflows.
opportunities.defensibility: A trusted answer system of record could compound, but the initial API is easy to copy.
opportunities.technical_feasibility: The API is straightforward if answer identity and freshness are modeled carefully.
opportunities.operational_burden: Integration maintenance and access support may grow quickly.

evidence_strength.rationale: Standalone API demand is not independently supported.

novelty.rationale: The source-of-truth wedge differs from a full questionnaire suite.

builder_fit.rationale: The prototype is small but integrations can expand scope.

technical_feasibility.rationale: Core API work is feasible while integrations add uncertainty.
